artifact: The top status-D districts are Hl.m. Praha with 4 status-D loans, Brno - mesto with 3, and Karvina with 3. They do not share a single socioeconomic signature: Praha has very low unemployment A13=0.43 but very high crime count A16=99,107 and salary A11=12,541; Karvina has high unemployment A13=7.75; Brno - mesto is closer to moderate unemployment A13=1.96. Compared with zero-D districts, the strongest common pattern is urban/high-volume and high crime count, not uniformly high unemployment.

sql.top_bottom_status_d_districts: WITH district_counts AS (SELECT d.district_id, d."A2" AS district, d."A3" AS region, d."A11" AS avg_salary, d."A13" AS unemployment_1996, d."A16" AS crime_count, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS loan_count, COUNT(l.loan_id) FILTER (WHERE l.status = 'D') AS status_d_count FROM district d LEFT JOIN account a ON a.district_id = d.district_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON l.account_id = a.account_id GROUP BY d.district_id, d."A2", d."A3", d."A11", d."A13", d."A16"), ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY status_d_count DESC, loan_count DESC) AS top_rank, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY status_d_count ASC, loan_count DESC) AS bottom_rank FROM district_counts) SELECT CASE WHEN top_rank <= 3 THEN 'top3_status_d' ELSE 'bottom3_status_d' END AS comparison_group, district, region, loan_count, status_d_count, avg_salary, unemployment_1996, crime_count FROM ranked WHERE top_rank <= 3 OR bottom_rank <= 3 ORDER BY comparison_group DESC, status_d_count DESC, loan_count DESC;
sql.district_status_d_scatter: SELECT d."A2" AS district, d."A3" AS region, d."A11" AS avg_salary, d."A13" AS unemployment_1996, d."A16" AS crime_count, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS loan_count, COUNT(l.loan_id) FILTER (WHERE l.status = 'D') AS status_d_count FROM district d LEFT JOIN account a ON a.district_id = d.district_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON l.account_id = a.account_id GROUP BY d.district_id, d."A2", d."A3", d."A11", d."A13", d."A16" ORDER BY status_d_count DESC, loan_count DESC;
